items.title: The Huge U.S. Bond Market And The U.S. Dollar Blow Off The 'Debasement Trade'
items.description: The dollar has bounced back some since the beginning of July with the WSJ Dollar Index rising today to 96.4, up by 2.6% from the low at the beginning of July.

items.description: All nine indexes on our world markets watch list have posted gains through October 13, 2025.

items.title: Consumer Debt Flatlined Again In August Signaling Financial Stress
items.description: After unexpectedly surging in July, consumer debt returned to its previous trend and flatlined again in August, signaling growing financial stress on American households.
